toolsUsed: System Events / AppKit / Carbon foreground attempts

assertions.title: Settings page shows scan directories

steps.action: Launched the native Tauri app directly on `/settings` with the isolated main fixture HOME and captured the rendered window plus the backing SQLite state.
steps.expected: The Settings page should list all built-in scan directories and any custom directories.
steps.observed: The saved settings-route evidence showed the page render but no built-in scan-directory path rows, and the isolated SQLite database still had an empty `scan_directories` table after startup, so the page had no seeded built-in directories to list.

assertions.issues: Failed because built-in scan directories were never populated into the `scan_directories` table, so the Settings scan-directory list could not satisfy the contract.

assertions.title: Add custom scan directory

steps.action: Attempted to delegate the interactive flow to a `user-testing-flow-validator` subagent, then tried to foreground and drive the native `/settings` window locally with System Events, AppKit/Carbon activation, and synthetic mouse/key events.
steps.expected: The Add Directory dialog should open so a custom path can be entered and rescanned.
steps.observed: The Task subagent exited immediately with `Exec ended early: insufficient permission to proceed`, and the native Tauri window stayed non-frontmost (`AXFrontmost` false, recursive AX tree only), so the Add Directory control could not be activated on the real user surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked by native Tauri window focus/input automation in this session before the Add Directory dialog could be opened.

assertions.title: Remove custom scan directory

steps.action: Tried to establish usable native input on the `/settings` window so an existing custom directory row could be removed.
steps.expected: A removable custom directory row should be operable so removing it triggers a rescan and removes the discovered skills.
steps.observed: The same native foreground/input blocker prevented any reliable click path to the Settings controls, so the remove flow could not be exercised on the real user surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked by the same native window focus/input issue that prevented the Add Directory flow.

assertions.title: Add custom platform

steps.action: Attempted to reach the custom-platform dialog through the native Settings page after the subagent delegation failure.
steps.expected: The Add Platform dialog should open so a custom platform name and directory can be submitted.
steps.observed: The native Tauri window never became interactive in this session, so the Add Platform control could not be activated on the real user surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked by native Tauri window focus/input automation in this session before the Add Platform dialog could be opened.

assertions.title: Custom platform shows skills

steps.action: Prepared a fixture custom-platform skills directory but first needed to drive the Add Platform UI so the app would register and rescan it.
steps.expected: After adding a custom platform and rescanning, its platform view should display the skills found in that directory.
steps.observed: Because the Add Platform interaction could not be executed on the real user surface, the prerequisite custom-platform registration never occurred and the downstream platform-view assertion could not be validated.

assertions.issues: Blocked by the untestable Add Platform flow in this session.

steps.action: Captured the native `/settings` route with the isolated main fixture HOME and inspected the About card text via OCR.
steps.expected: The Settings page should show the app version and database path.
steps.observed: The saved evidence clearly exposed `skills-manage v0.1.0` and `~/.skillsmanage/db.sqlite` in the About section of the real Tauri window.

assertions.title: Custom directory -> rescan -> visible

steps.action: Prepared an isolated custom-scan fixture directory and then attempted to open the Add Directory Settings flow needed to rescan it.
steps.expected: A user should be able to add the directory in Settings and then see the new skills appear after rescan.
steps.observed: The interactive Settings controls could not be reached on the real user surface because the native window never became foreground-interactive in this session.

assertions.issues: Blocked by the same native Settings input issue as VAL-SET-002.

assertions.title: Custom platform -> install -> verify

steps.action: Prepared an isolated custom-platform directory and importable central skills, then attempted to drive the Settings add-platform and install journey.
steps.expected: A user should be able to add a custom platform, install a central skill to it, and confirm the skill appears in that platform view.
steps.observed: The native window automation blocker prevented both the add-platform interaction and the subsequent install journey on the real user surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked by the same native Settings input issue as VAL-SET-004.

assertions.title: Navigation consistency

steps.action: Collected native evidence for the first-visit Central Skills route and the `/settings` route, then attempted to establish real sidebar/back-navigation interaction for the remaining views.
steps.expected: Every view should be reachable from the sidebar or navigation controls, and the sidebar should reflect the active view as a user navigates.
steps.observed: Direct route captures showed Central Skills and Settings rendering, but the real sidebar/back-navigation actions could not be driven because the native window would not become interactive in this session.

assertions.issues: Blocked by inability to exercise real sidebar/back-navigation interactions on the native Tauri surface.

assertions.title: Import collection from JSON

steps.action: Prepared a valid `imported-ops-collection.json` fixture under the isolated Downloads directory and then tried to reach the sidebar import control on the native app surface.
steps.expected: A user should be able to activate the import control, choose the JSON file, and see the imported collection created.
steps.observed: The native app window could be captured but not foregrounded or clicked in this session, so the import control could not even be opened on the real user surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked before the file-picker step because the native Tauri window was not interactable in this session.

frictions.description: Delegating to the `user-testing-flow-validator` Task subagent was not possible in this worker session.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the immediate `insufficient permission to proceed` failure and continued the validation manually inside the worker.

frictions.description: The native `skills-manage` window remained on-screen but never became foreground-interactive; accessibility exposure collapsed to recursive `AXApplication`/`AXMenuBar` nodes.
frictions.resolution: Captured the window by id with `screencapture -l`, extracted visible text with Apple Vision OCR, inspected the isolated SQLite database directly, and marked interaction-dependent assertions as blocked.

blockers.description: Native window foreground/input automation is blocked in this session: `AXFrontmost` remains false and synthetic input never lands on the Tauri window.
blockers.resolution: A future rerun needs a working foregrounding/input path for the native app window or another automation surface that can drive the real Tauri UI.

blockers.description: Built-in scan directories are not seeded into the `scan_directories` table on startup, so the Settings scan-directory list cannot satisfy VAL-SET-001.
blockers.resolution: Seed or synthesize the built-in scan-directory rows before `get_scan_directories` is used by the Settings page.

summary: Validated 10 settings-polish assertions as far as the current environment allowed. The Settings About section passed, built-in scan directories failed because no rows were ever seeded for the page to list, and the remaining interaction-driven assertions were blocked because the native Tauri window could be captured but not foregrounded or driven with real user input in this session.
